# ADR 0003: Central ApiConfiguration Registry for URI Templates

<!-- Generated by seed-golden-context | Last updated: 2026-05-11 -->

## Status

Accepted

## Context

Each CMA resource type maps to a URI template (`/spaces/{space}/environments/{environment}/entries/{entry}`), a set of required path parameters, a resource ID placeholder, and optionally a non-standard host (e.g. Upload API). Scattering these URI strings across individual resource or mapper classes would make them hard to audit, test, and change consistently.

## Decision

All URI template configuration lives in `src/ApiConfiguration.php` as a static associative array keyed by resource class name. `RequestUriBuilder` reads from this registry to construct concrete URLs at call time. The `host` key is used to override the base CMA URI for resources like `Upload` that target `upload.contentful.com`.

Evidence: `src/ApiConfiguration.php` (central `$configuration` array), `src/RequestUriBuilder.php`.

## Consequences

- Adding a new resource type requires a single entry in `ApiConfiguration` — the rest of the dispatch machinery works automatically.
- The full API surface is auditable in one file.
- Programmatic tests can validate URI shapes without making live HTTP calls.
- The registry is PHP class-name–keyed, so resource renames require updating both the class and the configuration entry.
